title: B12 deficiency endocrine review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports variable and nonspecific symptom context, neurological, cognitive, visual, fatigue, anaemia, diet, medicine, surgery and other risk-factor documentation.
assessment.rationale: The exact recognition section supports onset, interval change, functional impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and patient concerns for a B12-deficiency review without confirming deficiency or cause.

source.title: Vitamin B12 deficiency in over 16s: diagnosis and management
source.population: People aged 16 years and older with suspected or confirmed vitamin B12 deficiency, with pregnancy, breastfeeding, medicine, surgery and cause-specific qualifiers.
source.setting: Primary and specialist assessment, testing, cause evaluation, ongoing care and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected symptom, risk-factor, result-context, medicine-context and follow-up documentation. It does not infer deficiency, select treatment or establish UAE laboratory thresholds or pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.6 and Boxes 1–2 — recognition, symptoms and risk factors

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports variable and nonspecific symptom context, neurological, cognitive, visual, fatigue, anaemia, diet, medicine, surgery and other risk-factor documentation.

assessment.text: Supports recording the type of vitamin B12 test, pregnancy or nitrous-oxide context, current supplements, laboratory reference context, indeterminate results and clinician-led further assessment without autonomously interpreting a result.
assessment.rationale: The exact test and result-context section supports focused assessment and clinician-reviewed laboratory documentation, including supplement and assay context, without autonomously interpreting the result.

source.title: Vitamin B12 deficiency in over 16s: diagnosis and management

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.16 — tests, result context and interpretation factors

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording the type of vitamin B12 test, pregnancy or nitrous-oxide context, current supplements, laboratory reference context, indeterminate results and clinician-led further assessment without autonomously interpreting a result.

assessment.text: Supports symptom response, worsening or new symptoms, adherence context and clinician-agreed reassessment or follow-up. Medicine route, dose and frequency are excluded from mappings.
assessment.rationale: The exact follow-up section supports recording worsening or new symptoms, clinician concern, a clinician-entered plan, discussed safety net and arranged follow-up; medicine route and dose remain unsupported.

source.title: Vitamin B12 deficiency in over 16s: diagnosis and management
source.population: People aged 16 years and older with suspected or confirmed vitamin B12 deficiency, with pregnancy, breastfeeding, medicine, surgery and cause-specific qualifiers.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports symptom response, worsening or new symptoms, adherence context and clinician-agreed reassessment or follow-up. Medicine route, dose and frequency are excluded from mappings.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-documented autoimmune-gastritis, coeliac, medicine, diet, surgery and other cause-review context without asserting a cause.
assessment.rationale: Cause-identification recommendations were reviewed, but no cause is inferred or inserted.
